halfLife.conditions: mean elimination half-life across 146 healthy volunteers (label)

vd.conditions: apparent volume V/F = (CL/F)/ke = (53 mL/min)/(ln2 / 8.3 h) ≈ 38.1 L, using the label's apparent oral clearance CL/F ≈ 53 mL/min; the label reports no volume of distribution. F set to 1 (apparent-volume convention)

F.conditions: set to 1 by the apparent-volume convention; the absolute oral bioavailability is not reported in this label

tmax.conditions: time to peak ≈ 1 hour (label; rapidly absorbed)

fda_label.title: Cetirizine Hydrochloride Oral Solution (Bajaj Medical, LLC) — FDA Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology

notes: Curated from the FDA Structured Product Label for cetirizine hydrochloride oral solution (Bajaj Medical). The cited adult pharmacokinetic values — half-life 8.3 h (146 volunteers), Tmax ≈ 1 h, apparent oral clearance CL/F ≈ 53 mL/min, 93% plasma protein binding — are the systemic parameters reported in that label's clinical-pharmacology section and apply across formulations. The label does NOT report a volume of distribution, so the apparent volume V/F is DERIVED as V/F = (CL/F)/ke = (53 mL/min)/(ln2 / 8.3 h) ≈ 38.1 L; it is marked derived and carries the 'derived_from_clearance' provenance sentinel. CL/F is deliberately NOT stored as a structured field: it has no role beyond producing this Vd, and storing it would make the engine recompute ke = CL/Vd — a circular cross-check that would falsely read as an independent confirmation. Instead ke is taken straight from the half-life (ke = ln2/8.3 h), so the plotted terminal slope matches the stated 8.3 h exactly. F is set to 1 under the apparent-volume convention (the absolute bioavailability is not stated in this label), so the plotted curve reflects C = F·D/V = D/(V/F) without double-counting F. PK is linear over oral doses of 5–60 mg (label), so superposition is valid in the therapeutic range. Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
